urgency.description: Creates a general sense of urgency.
Strong pressure to act without delay.
Pressures the user into replying quickly.
Creates fear of missing a deadline.
Creates urgency through an approaching deadline.
Uses fear to force quick action.
Claims the email is highly important.
Claims a critical change requiring action.
Suggests an issue with the user's account.
Claims the offer or action expires today.
Creates urgency using a fixed deadline.
Discourages the user from ignoring the email.
Uses fear of missing an opportunity.
Uses scarcity to encourage quick decisions.
Sets a same-day deadline to pressure the recipient.
Demands instant completion of a task.
Creates pressure using a same-day deadline.
Uses an extremely short response window.
Demands confirmation as quickly as possible.
Strong urgency cue frequently used in phishing.
Insists on completion before the end of the day.
Creates pressure with a one-day deadline.
Pressures users with a stated deadline.
Uses fear of losing an opportunity.
Claims an active session is about to end.
Suggests the user has already missed a deadline.
Claims a required response is overdue.
Direct command intended to rush the recipient.
Uses fear to discourage careful thinking.
Requires action before the day ends.
Strong demand for an instant reply.
Creates urgency using a short remaining timeframe.
Warns that a deadline is very close.
Threatens account closure unless action is taken.
Uses fear of losing account access.
Claims account access will soon expire.
Requests completion before a specified deadline.
Attempts to make responding seem compulsory.
Suggests action is blocked until approval.
Threatens loss of access to pressure immediate action.

credential.description: Requests the user to log in.

payment.description: Creates urgency around an overdue payment.
Claims a refund has been approved.
Requests action to receive a refund.

personal_info.description: Attempts to obtain sensitive personal information.

crypto.description: Requests or references a crypto wallet.

reward.description: Claims the recipient has won something.
Declares the recipient as a winner.
Claims the recipient won a prize.

threat.description: Claims the account has been suspended.
Claims the user's account is suspended.
Claims the account has been disabled.
Threatens consequences for ignoring the message.
Claims the account is at risk.
Claims user access has been revoked.
Claims the user has been blacklisted.
Claims the account has been blocked.
